rationale: In the citing paper, Hildebrand et al. (2014) appears as part of a grouped citation supporting a general introductory claim that sequential configuration choices can be onerous for consumers. The paper does not single out this source for a distinct theoretical mechanism, hypothesis, measure, or empirical benchmark, and it is not meaningfully revisited in the core analyses. Given this usage, the citation functions as background context rather than a substantive building block.
